The database schema uses PostgreSQL with twelve normalized tables. The users table has a primary key on id with a unique index on email. Foreign keys link orders to users and order_items to orders with cascading deletes. We added a composite index on orders(user_id, created_at) to speed up the dashboard query that was doing a sequential scan. The migration strategy uses numbered SQL files applied in order: each migration is idempotent and wrapped in a transaction. Schema changes go through a review process where the DBA checks for missing indexes, N+1 query risks, and lock contention on high-traffic tables. We recently migrated the sessions table from JSON columns to a dedicated key-value structure, which cut query latency by forty percent.
